name: Electronic Market Making (Quote-Driven HFT)
description: Post simultaneous bid and ask quotes around the theoretical fair value of an asset. Profit from the bid-ask spread when orders hit both sides. Manage inventory to avoid directional exposure. Cancel and requote in microseconds as fair value changes. Uses ultra-low-latency infrastructure (co-location, FPGA).
core_logic: fair_value = mid_price (NBBO). bid = fair_value - spread/2. ask = fair_value + spread/2. Inventory model (Avellaneda-Stoikov): adjust quotes toward mean-reverting inventory target. Cancel all quotes if volatility spikes.

risk.main_risk: Adverse selection (being picked off by informed traders)

signal_buy: Post bid quotes when spread environment > 2x target spread
signal_sell: Post ask quotes simultaneously (both sides always quoted)
